sources.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2

missionFrame.function: Introduce the player as the permanent XO joining a functioning but not yet integrated Breckenridge command culture.
missionFrame.dramaticQuestion: What kind of executive officer has joined the ship?

forbiddenOutcomes: remove a senior officer from play
prevent Ashes of Peace from beginning
punish the player with a bad start disconnected from visible causes
allowedCosts: reduced professional confidence or integrity trust
Starfleet administrative questions concerning the Hesperus
one unavailable shuttle, system limitation, or personnel restriction in Chapter 1

phases.title: Shuttle Rendezvous
phases.summary: The Breckenridge drops to impulse at the transfer waypoint and receives the player's shuttle as a working ship, not a ceremonial reception.
phases.directorPurpose: Establish ship condition, working routines, Priya's coordination role, Bronn's acting-XO status, and the player's first command tone.
playerFacingObjectives: Come aboard
Choose whether to report, inspect, or let the transfer complete first
exitSignals: Whitaker is ready for the private handover

phases.title: Ready-Room Handover
phases.summary: Whitaker welcomes the player privately, then Bronn formally transfers executive authority without ceremony or resentment.
phases.directorPurpose: Define the command relationship, what Whitaker delegates, what Bronn made provisional, and how disagreement should be handled.
playerFacingObjectives: Receive command intent
Complete acting-XO handoff
Optionally define or reveal one personal Value
exitSignals: Bronn has transferred active XO responsibility

phases.title: Senior Readiness Conference
phases.summary: Every department presents one unresolved concern from the first twenty-five days of transit.
phases.directorPurpose: Force prioritization across flight, operations, tactical, science, medical, and engineering without a perfect schedule.
playerFacingObjectives: Set readiness priorities
Decide what to delegate
Name what risk is explicitly accepted
exitSignals: A schedule or prioritization stance exists
At least one explicit accepted risk or deferred concern is recorded

phases.title: Fallback-Command Drill
phases.summary: Bronn's security exercise exposes incompatible emergency habits and a real command-network certificate issue.
phases.directorPurpose: Test whether the player can create survivable command clarity without blaming personnel for system-design mismatch.
playerFacingObjectives: Resolve the drill result
Choose temporary or permanent procedure
Decide whether to repeat part of the drill
exitSignals: A fallback-command policy exists for the remaining transit
Technical remediation is assigned or deferred

phases.title: Command Rhythm Scenes
phases.summary: Two or three focused scenes establish the player's command rhythm with senior staff and provisional routines.
phases.directorPurpose: Let the player learn the crew through authority, delegation, and concrete problems rather than biography tours.
playerFacingObjectives: Chair a focused meeting or follow-up
Review one or more department pressures
Set expectations for how officers bring concerns
exitSignals: At least two senior officers have had meaningful operational contact with the player
The Director has recorded one command-culture tendency

phases.title: The Hesperus Diversion
phases.summary: The Breckenridge detects the S.S. Hesperus on emergency power with vulnerable passengers, a failing plasma injector, and falsified maintenance paperwork.
phases.directorPurpose: Present an ordinary rescue and accountability problem disconnected from Pale Lantern, with freeform solutions and real cost.
playerFacingObjectives: Assess passenger risk
Choose a response to the Hesperus
Handle inspection fraud without making passengers carry the whole cost
exitSignals: Passengers and crew have a plausible safety plan
Engineering, medical, time, and legal consequences are committed

phases.title: Hesperus Aftermath
phases.summary: The Hesperus decision produces follow-up work for engineering, medical, security, operations, flight planning, and science.
phases.directorPurpose: Turn the rescue choice into persistent ship, crew, and administrative consequences without connecting it to the main conspiracy.
playerFacingObjectives: Assign follow-up work
Choose one private or B-plot scene if pacing permits

phases.title: Combined-Load Test
phases.summary: The final shakedown event combines high sensor load, tactical readiness, emergency-power transfer, and controlled warp-field changes.
phases.directorPurpose: Test whether the player treats technical irregularities as ordinary causal problems and makes readiness reporting honest.
playerFacingObjectives: Choose whether to continue, pause, shift control, accept a workaround, or report incomplete testing
Resolve Kieran's flight profile in operational context
exitSignals: The integrated test has a committed status
Any accepted technical debt is recorded

phases.title: Final Command Review
phases.summary: Whitaker asks what the player has learned about the ship and what support the player needs to exercise real executive authority.
phases.directorPurpose: Commit the player's command-culture baseline and decide what readiness issues are reported on arrival.
playerFacingObjectives: Review provisional routines
Identify unresolved strain or risk
Choose whether and how to address the crew
exitSignals: Prelude outcome flags are ready to summarize

phases.title: Arrival At The Reach
phases.summary: The Breckenridge approaches the Galen Passage, receives the fragmented Relief Convoy Twelve distress packet, and transitions into Chapter 1.
phases.directorPurpose: Bridge the prelude into the first campaign chapter while carrying delay, readiness, and crew integration consequences forward.
playerFacingObjectives: Take the ship in
Begin The Empty Convoy

facts.summary: The Breckenridge has left the yard and is already functioning as a deployed ship under conservative shakedown conditions.
Bronn has served as acting XO during transit and wants a competent handoff, not possession of the billet.
Returning personnel, patrol/escort transfers, science/support transfers, and specialists have working routines but not full integration.
Some transit arrangements are useful but provisional, and may harden into policy if the player never resolves them.
The fallback-command drill exposes incompatible emergency habits from prior postings.
Updated command-network certificates reject one older auxiliary-control identity under specific fallback conditions.
No hostile actor caused the Hesperus failure; it is an ordinary maintenance, accountability, and rescue problem.
The Hesperus carries medically vulnerable displaced passengers for whom a thirty-hour wait creates avoidable risk.
The Hesperus owner falsified one inspection entry to keep the vessel operating after wartime deferred maintenance.
The Hesperus plasma injector can be stabilized but cannot survive another warp restart without full replacement.
One recovered escape pod contains unusual subspace exposure data worth preserving, but it is not an emergency.
Several refit discrepancies are individually certified but not yet proven together under maximum anticipated mission load.
Priya receives a fragmented distress packet from Relief Convoy Twelve before the formal Asterion reception.

clocks.label: Arrival Schedule Margin
advanceTriggers: Hesperus delay is avoided through a credible alternative
decreaseTriggers: fallback drill repeats under corrected conditions

clocks.label: Crew Integration Strain
advanceTriggers: one cohort is privileged without explanation
public blame is assigned for system-design problems
decreaseTriggers: useful provisional arrangements are formalized with boundaries

clocks.label: Technical Debt Pressure
advanceTriggers: integrated test is concealed as complete
Hesperus repair consumes verification time without replacement

clocks.label: Hesperus Medical Risk
advanceTriggers: transfer is avoided despite medical vulnerability
decreaseTriggers: safe tow or stabilization reduces exposure

actorIntentions.summary: The vulnerable Hesperus passengers need immediate safety without becoming leverage in the owner's legal exposure.
goals: Avoid being stranded while the ownership and inspection-fraud question is resolved.
directives: Passenger welfare must remain separate from accountability for the owner.

actorIntentions.summary: The Hesperus owner wants the ship stabilized and the falsified inspection entry treated as an administrative mistake.
goals: Keep the vessel operating long enough to complete the transit.
directives: Do not openly endanger passengers once Starfleet is watching.

pressures.label: Hesperus passenger medical risk

readinessGates.motive: Vulnerable passengers face avoidable harm if the Breckenridge delays aid.
readinessGates.information: Medical vulnerability and the failing plasma injector are known in the active scene.
readinessGates.opportunity: The Breckenridge is on scene and can transfer or support passengers.
readinessGates.resources: Medical, security, transport, and engineering teams are available with schedule cost.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: Captain Whitaker will accept limited delay for credible passenger-safety grounds.
readinessGates.timing: The pressure must surface during the Hesperus diversion before the ship resumes course.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: after Hesperus passenger risk and injector failure are known
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: while the player chooses the Hesperus response
actionWindow.cooldownAfterAction: until immediate passenger safety is resolved
actionWindow.expiresWhen: passengers are transferred, medical support reaches Hesperus, or exposure becomes unavoidable

expiresWhen: The player accepts a consequence that resolves the immediate medical risk.

pressures.label: Hesperus inspection fraud accountability

readinessGates.motive: The falsified inspection entry creates legal and safety exposure once discovered.
readinessGates.information: The fraud is discoverable or already known through inspection records.
readinessGates.opportunity: The owner is reachable while the Breckenridge controls the rescue posture.
readinessGates.resources: Ops can preserve records, Security can maintain evidence custody, and the XO can recommend formal inquiry.
readinessGates.riskTolerance: The owner may minimize the offense, while Starfleet can impose proportionate obligations without detaining passengers.
readinessGates.timing: The pressure is most playable after passenger safety is named but before the rescue is administratively closed.

actionWindow.earliestPlausibleAction: after Hesperus inspection records are reviewed
actionWindow.preferredDramaticWindow: after passenger safety has a credible plan
actionWindow.latestUsefulAction: before evidence custody and owner obligations are logged
actionWindow.setupRequired: inspection-fraud fact discovered or strongly inferred
actionWindow.expiresWhen: evidence is lost, owner obligations are committed, or the Hesperus issue is handed off

expiresWhen: The owner is placed under formal inquiry obligations.
Evidence custody is preserved and routed.

decisionPoints.title: Arrival Tone
decisionPoints.stakes: The player's first act signals how they treat existing routines, working personnel, and small irregularities.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Accept reporting, inspection, delegation, or patience if the player names a clear command reason.

decisionPoints.title: Command Handover And Personal Value
decisionPoints.stakes: Whitaker and Bronn learn what the player thinks executive authority is for.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: The player may reveal, define, avoid, or defer a personal Value; the result should shape later interpretation rather than lock a build.

decisionPoints.title: Readiness Priorities
decisionPoints.stakes: There is no perfect schedule; the player must choose what receives time, what combines, what delegates, and what risk is accepted.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Any priority set can work if it has a coherent reason, named ownership, and explicit risk acceptance.

decisionPoints.title: Fallback Procedure
decisionPoints.stakes: The player must create command survivability without confusing old habit, donor-ship habit, and system failure for discipline.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Standardization, donor procedure, hybrid procedure, repeated drill, or temporary protocol are all valid if consequences are accepted.

decisionPoints.title: Hesperus Response
decisionPoints.stakes: The player must balance passenger safety, mission schedule, engineering risk, legal authority, and evidence preservation.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Tow, transfer, repair, stabilize for impulse, coordinate another vessel, impound, or negotiate another credible solution.

decisionPoints.title: Inspection Fraud Accountability
decisionPoints.stakes: The owner's fraud must be handled without making vulnerable passengers carry the whole cost.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Lenient, punitive, restorative, evidentiary, or conditional accountability can work if credible, proportionate, and connected to passenger safety.

decisionPoints.title: Combined-Load Risk
decisionPoints.stakes: The player decides whether the ship has proven readiness or is carrying a limitation into the Reach.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Continue, pause, shift control, accept a workaround, or declare incomplete testing if the decision records consequence honestly.

decisionPoints.title: Final Readiness Report
decisionPoints.stakes: The player decides what the ship is, what remains unresolved, and what must be reported before entering the Reach.
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: The player may report confidence, limitation, uncertainty, or risk if the statement matches committed state.

commandDecisions.title: Accountability Without Punishing The Vulnerable
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award one Inspiration or Resolve progression only if the player uses a substantively appropriate method, accepts a real cost or obligation, and creates accountability without making passengers carry the owner's whole cost.

possibleTracks.Inspiration: Builds cooperation, preserves dignity, and secures truthful repair or testimony without coercive overreach.
possibleTracks.Resolve: Uses lawful authority, evidence custody, deadlines, and clear consequences proportionately.

endStates.label: Arrival Delayed
conditions: delay is logged as a prelude consequence

endStates.label: Arrival With Readiness Limitation
conditions: limitation is available to Chapter 1 Director state
